headline: Token pricing pressure leads the window as AI IPOs and inference-time compute costs come into focus.
body: The strongest pull this window is toward AI cost and pricing: TechCrunch frames upcoming AI IPOs as a driver of token price increases, raising the prospect of a "Tokenpocalypse," while separate analyses tally AI capital expenditure against hypothetical returns [0][1][6][7]. On the technique side, prompt caching and test-time scaling are both breaking out, surfacing in a discussion of how tunable thinking efforts in Claude and ChatGPT control inference-time computation and trigger cache-breaking when changed mid-conversation [11]. Model release and API also register as breakouts, with OpenAI continuing work on a "super app" and a senior employee claiming chat as a dominant interface is ending [5]. Cost measurement, training-free methods, and product strategy enter as new concepts, rounding out a window focused on the economics and inference mechanics of running models at scale.

cited_items.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
Billions spent and hypothetical returns: the AI boom explained with six charts
OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
The OnlyFans Economy of American AI
